provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Petroleum Products Marking Regulations, 2005 and shall be deemed to have come into operation on the 31 st day of December, 2004.

provisions.title: Application
provisions.content: The provisions of these Regulations shall apply throughout Tanzania Mainland and Tanzania Zanzibar.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these Regulations unless the context requires otherwise:— “ approved marker ” means the chemical substance recognized by an antibody and approved by the Government Chemist to be used by the marking company to mark petroleum products ; “ dealer ” means an oil marketing company, petrol station, transporter or any other person who engages himself in the business of importing, supplying, distributing, transporting or selling petroleum products ; “ duty and tax free petroleum products ” means petroleum products , which are consumed by duty and tax exempted persons; “ export petroleum products ” means petroleum products which are imported into the country and exported or transited to neighbouring countries; “ marking company ” means a company contracted or designated by the Tanzania Association of Oil Marketing Companies and approved by Commissioner for Customs and Excise to povide petroleum products marking services; “ marking terminals ” means the nominated sites designated for marking of petroleum products ; “ Minister ” means Minister responsible for finance; “ petroleum products for domestic use ” means petroleum products which are taxed “ petroleum products ” means petrol, diesel and kerosene; “ proper officer ” has the meaning ascribed to it under section 3 of the the East African Customs and Transfer Tax Management Act, 1970. [Cap. 27 of the Laws of the Community]

provisions.title: Payment of duties and taxes on petroleum products
provisions.content: (1) All petroleum products imported for domestic use shall be charged customs duties according to the procedure provided under subregulation (2) . (2) A dealer importing petroleum products for domestic use shall on importation, pay all relevant customs duties in respect of the imported petroleum products . (3) In case a dealer fails to comply with the provisions of subregulation (1) , he shall be obliged to pay fifty percent of the customs duties due within a period of fifteen days from the date of importation and the remaining fifty percent of the duties due shall be paid thereafter in two equal installments so that the whole liability is discharged within a period of forty five days from the date of importation. (4) For purposes of ensuring that payment of duties is made in accordance with the provisions of this regulation, a dealer importing petroleum products shall within a period of twenty one days prior to actual importation, give notice in writing to the proper officer of the intended importation (5) A dealer who contravenes any of the provisions of this regulation, commits an offence and shall be liable to conviction to pay a sum of money equal to Tanzania Shillings two million.

provisions.title: Marking of petroleum products
provisions.content: (1) All petroleum products for domestic use and for export together with duty and tax free petroleum product shall be marked with approved markers. (2) The marking company shall mark all petroleum products for domestic use and for export together with duty and tax free petroleum products with the approved markers. (3) Marking of petroleum products shall be done at all marking terminals and any place as may be approved by the Commissioner for Customs and Excise. (4) A dealer shall ensure that all petroleum products in his possession are marked with appropriate approved marker and that the marking company is informed of all movements of petroleum. (5) The marking company shall ensure that its marking, sampling and testing procedures arc well known to dealers.

provisions.title: Test and examination
provisions.content: (1) An authorized officer of the marking company take samples of petroleum products from retail petrol stations, non retail consumers, oil marketing companies bunkers and other points selected, and may carry out tests and examinations to determine levels of the markers in the samples. (2) The samples required under sub-regulation (1) shall be supplied by the dealer to the authorized officer free of charge. (3) The results of any test or examination conducted under these Regulations shall be made available to the Commissioner for Customs and Excise and the concerned dealer .

provisions.title: Fees
provisions.content: The dealer shall pay the marking company a marking fee of USD 1.2 per cubic meter of petroleum products marked of its equivalent in Tanzania Shillings which shall be collected by Tanzania Association of Oil Marketing Companies.

provisions.title: Diluted and adulterated petroleum products
provisions.content: Where a test or examination of a sample taken under regulation 6 indicates— (a) a dilution of the marker below the accepted standard of ninety percent or over marking of the marker above the accepted standard of one hundred and ten percent; or (b) a product of a different type or grade specification has been added to the petroleum products , then the provisions of customs laws relating to goods liable to forfeiture shall apply.

provisions.title: Offence and penalty on breach of marking regulations
provisions.content: Where a dealer or any other person is found in possession of petroleum products mentioned in regulation 8(a) and (b) , he shall be deemed to be in possession of goods liable to forfeiture and shall be guilty of an offence and liable to a fine not exceeding one hundred percent of the value of the petroleum products in question.

provisions.title: Revocation of GN. No. 45 of 2001
provisions.content: The Petroleum Marking Regulations, 2001 are hereby revoked.
